questions.question: In digital logic design, what is the primary function of a multiplexer (MUX)?
options: To combine multiple inputs into a single output based on a select signal.
To split a single input into multiple outputs.
To perform arithmetic operations on two inputs.
To store a single bit of data.
To generate clock signals for synchronous circuits.

questions.question: In the physical design phase of an Application-Specific Integrated Circuit (ASIC), what is the primary purpose of "floorplanning"?
options: To perform static timing analysis on the synthesized netlist.
To convert the Register-Transfer Level (RTL) code into a gate-level netlist.
To define the overall chip dimensions, aspect ratio, and preliminary placement of major functional blocks (e.g., CPU core, memory macros, I/O pads).
To route all the power and ground lines across the entire chip.
To generate test vectors for post-manufacturing testing.

questions.question: When implementing clock gating as a power reduction technique in digital ICs, what is the most critical design consideration to prevent functional errors?
options: Ensuring the gated clock signal has a precise 50% duty cycle.
Preventing glitches or undesired toggles on the gated clock signal.
Minimizing the number of flip-flops connected to the gated clock.
Ensuring the clock enable signal is synchronous to the ungated clock.
Maximizing the propagation delay of the clock gating cell.

questions.question: In the modern digital IC design flow, what is the primary role of Hardware Description Languages (HDLs) like Verilog or VHDL?
options: To perform physical layout and routing of transistors.
To verify the analog behavior of mixed-signal circuits.
To synthesize power distribution networks.
To describe the functionality and structure of digital circuits at various levels of abstraction, from behavioral to Register-Transfer Level (RTL).
To simulate the thermal characteristics of the silicon substrate.

questions.question: In computer architecture, particularly concerning memory hierarchies and caching, what does the principle of "temporal locality" primarily describe?
options: The tendency for a program to access memory locations that are physically close to recently accessed locations.
The phenomenon where a program accesses the same memory location multiple times within a short period.
The ability of a processor to prefetch data into cache before it is explicitly requested.
The amount of time a memory block remains in the cache before being evicted.
The speed at which data can be transferred between the CPU and main memory.
